mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 4.0% signals labor market slack that reduces consumer spending growth and corporate profit margins, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 4.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance with p > 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and consumer price inflation indicate strong aggregate demand outpacing supply constraints, which forces equity markets to price in higher corporate revenues and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in >55% of triggered cases, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production signals expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which triggers short-term positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days during economic expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns during expansion periods, with a hit rate below 50% or a decayed autocorrelation structure invalidating the short-term signal.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust labor market absorption and rising household income, which sustains consumer expenditure and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth correlates negatively with 20-day forward returns in >60% of observations, or the predictive IC falls below 0.01 across multiple rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts dropping below 1.2 million units signals a sharp contraction in construction activity and downstream consumer durables demand, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >65% of periods when HOUST falls below 1200.0, or the threshold effect disappears when controlling for interest rate changes.

mechanism_narrative: A widening gap between rising federal funds rates and accelerating consumer price inflation indicates monetary policy tightening that fails to anchor inflation expectations, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a positive predictive coefficient, or the combined signal fails to outperform a baseline model with a t-statistic below 1.5 over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product exceeding 2.0% confirms sustained macroeconomic expansion, which supports corporate profit growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days during stable policy environments.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth above 2.0% coincides with negative 60-day forward returns in >60% of cases, or the signal reverses direction during periods of high market volatility.
